item.title: Shitty Monday, have a Meme dump
item.description:
Where's my Boi, Beaker
Sound on
My best friend's daughter had a conference with her dolls on a sick day where doctor Barbie called for free health care and Ken asked for more beach days, so all the Barbies were like yes okay boy, anyway, health care.
I know for sure I have the same exasperated groan as my mom and grandma. Wonder how far back it goes. Somewhere in Holland is a distant cousin going, "aaaaagh".
Be brave enough to murder something new. In the garden. Not people. For legal reasons I do not condone homicide.
Asking Colm Meany this was tbh a stupid question lol
My dog sent her cousin's toy to Valhalla yesterday. Feast well, drum-wielding T-rex.
Edgar Allan Poe. And Diogenes of Sinope.
International treasure. Once in college we were watching a black and white film of a stage production of Agamemnon. 20m in I screamed SIR PAT STEW HAS HAIR!!!
I've gotten the hang of bread. Only thing worse is meringue cookies. If your humidity is over 40% and you don't have alcohol based flavorings, just toss it now.
Tbh, me too, and when I tried to relate this to my bro in law, he didn't get it and it made me sad. If you don't have a food you enjoy enough that it makes you dance in place, how awful.
Say it with me now: Lawns are stupid.
My 13yo niece is a hard-core JP fan
For the love of God, Montressor!!!!! Once when helping my dad install drywall I went behind the sheet and yelled that. He slowly leaned around it, and couldn't keep a straight face while telling me to stop that.
Also teams. We have ONE dept at work that likes it. Everyone else uses Google suites. It has issues too but didn't render a meeting lead mute for 20m.
Me teaching the history of psychology
Spent 10m today lamenting with a friend about early onset arthritis.
Rip the suspension on my dad's truck that I borrowed in the snow today ?
See also Xanthippe
My sister always suggests if you need to call in or cancel anything just cite raging diarrhea. Nobody will ask any questions to follow up.
This is why I take my aunt to our school production of Shakespeare every year
Cog sucker
Live long, eat bread, love hard
Love doing 2 step authentication to get to my 2 step authentication so I can complete my 2 step authentication and turn in attendance for class.
Dog tax: today entails a lot of violet standing on top of her cousin Hayley demanding to play.
item.dc:creator: AnxiousCuttlefish

item.title: 20 nice and wholesome things to brighten your day, vol 200
item.description:
A woman’s voice activates the same part of a man’s brain as hearing music does.
I barbecue often, and when I do I always bring in the best bit I can to my wife; regardless of how late at night it is. It’s gotten to the point where she loves me barbecuing, because she knows delicious treats are close at hand.

item.title: The 1936 Illinois Occupational Diseases Act was written in the radium-tainted blood of The Radium Girls
item.description:
There is a famous Tumblr post that goes around about how companies prioritize profit over lives: “Your regulations are written in blood.” The Radium Girls are one of the biggest testaments to that. Between 1917 - 1930, female factory workers were exposed to dangerous amounts of radium without warning or protections. One by one, they each died of crippling cancers and bone decay. Many of them didn’t live past thirty. The few that did only did so because they weren’t employed as long as the others. However, before we can get into the plight of the Radium Girls, we need to go over what radium is and how it works. That's what Part 1 is for.
The Tumblr post I mentioned.
One of the things that made a large difference in WW1 was the ability to read military equipment in the dark. This was made possible due to the discovery of radium, and the invention of radium-infused paints. You’d take a regular dial for a watch or airplane meter, and very delicately paint over the numbers with radium. It created a brilliant luminous glow that could be seen even in the pitch dark.
We’ll come back around to the radium girls themselves later. First, it’s important to understand the substance they were exposed to.
I don’t have the most thorough grasp on how radioactivity works. I’ve tried many times to understand it, and the closest I got was a physicist telling me “it’s like shooting a thin sheet of cardboard with an AK-47.” Unfortunately, he got really creepy with me, and I had to cut ties with him before I could get the full scoop on radiation. This is only going to focus on why radiation can deteriorate bone, but it DOES require a small refresher, starting with atoms.
Ionizing radiation are radioactive substances that emit enough energy to break molecular bonds, and knock off electrons from an atom. Basically, it can physically change the chemical composition of matter in its path. This includes DNA! This is why radiation exposure can lead to genetic mutations.
One of the reasons that radiation therapy is used for cancer treatment is because radiation energy doesn't stop until it's deteriorated. The simple concept of this: applying radiation to cancer means that the radiation will continue to barrage the area with high energy until it's been destroyed. There's obviously more steps to using radiation as treatment, but I'm just giving a general idea of how radiation is used. There's also different types of radiation you have to look at.
With all of the above being said, radioactive substances can affect different things. Everything it touches is contaminated, but that doesn’t mean it will automatically be reduced to nothing. Radium, for example, doesn’t disintegrate paper. In fact, Marie Curie’s scientific journals are still in relatively good condition and are held in a museum in France. However, they are also so radioactive that being exposed to them without protection will cause serious damage to bone and tissue, as those are the molecular structures that the gamma waves break. Now let's look at radium.
Radium was discovered in 1898 by renowned scientist, Marie Curie, and her husband Pierre Curie. They were originally studying and testing pitchblende, which is a radioactive ore that’s rich in uranium. During testing, they discovered another radioactive metal, which they named polonium, after their homeland, Poland. While testing polonium further, Marie noticed a substance that was four times as radioactive as both uranium and polonium in their samples. That was radium. Radium is to date the most naturally occurring radioactive metal on Earth. It was also enchanting due to its luminous glow, which is how it got its name, radium. Radium was derived from the word “radiant.”
There was actually a time where people thought that radium was good for you! There were tonics with radium mixed into it, and all sorts of products that had (or at least claimed to have) traces of radium in it. One of these products was Radithor, which was radium-infused water. This was a popular drink among the wealthy, as they believed that it would make them live longer. It was also prescribed by doctors for ailments like headaches and weakness. Unfortunately, radium is a "bone seeking" element, meaning it will target and annihilate your bones if consumed.
Just a few more products, including hand cream and butter. Fortunately, many of these products were only claiming to contain radium because the actual substance was extremely difficult to get. It’s one of those really rare occasions where greed and dishonesty actually saved a swindled customer.
On the periodic table of elements, you’ll notice that calcium and radium are both in the category of “alkaline earth metals.” Radium and calcium are obviously not exact, but their composition is very similar to one another. Remember that our bodies strengthen our bones with calcium. When radium is ingested, your body will mistake it for calcium and send it to the bones. Eventually the gamma rays will erode the bone from the inside out until it’s too deteriorated to maintain its structure. Essentially, the calcium in the bones is replaced with radium - a highly destructive radioactive substance. If consumed, the bone will eventually disintegrate until it can’t maintain its structure anymore. Because radium was often consumed via oral consumption, these damages were especially pronounced in the jaw. In many sufferers of radium poisoning, the mandible would erode to the point where it would just fall off the skull and crumble.
You might be wondering about how a person's jaw just falls off of their skull? The skull is in two pieces. and only held together by the temporomandibular joint. There's a condition called temporomandibular joint dysfunction (TMJD, though it's often shortened to TMJ) where problems with the ligaments, cartilage disk, and muscles around this joint create problems like lockjaw, facial structure changes, and terrible jaw pain. That's for a different day. In this case, the bone of the TMJ was literally crumbling to pieces. Eventually the bone density of the TMJ just wasn't enough to hold the skull together, and the jaw fell out.
Mollie Maggia was the first radium girl to die from radium poisoning. We’ll officially meet her in part 2. A week before her death, she had gone to the dentist. To their horror, when the dentist gently examined her jaw, the entire mandible came off. He said: “I removed her jawbone, not by an operation, but merely by putting my fingers in her mouth and lifting it out […] whenever a portion of the affected bone was removed, instead of arresting the course of the necrosis, it sped it up” He noted the “honeycomb structure” of the jaw, showing that the bone had been severely eroded from the inside out. It didn't just apply to jaw bones, however. Spines, legs, necks, teeth, arms, and any body part that involved a bone could be eroded by radium. Some of the radium girls were in rigid back braces towards the end of their life due to the extreme deterioration of their spines and legs. You can see some of the deterioration in that x-ray. . I included the other jaw bone because it came from an official dentist's website, and it showed the same effect. The only thing that has me slightly incredulous is the fact that the remaining teeth appear to be in good condition, which wasn't the case for many of the radium girls. I decided to include it with a disclaimer for that reason.
There are three reasons consuming radium became a health fad, though it could be broken down to two like in the meme. . 1.) A GOOD CANCER TREATMENT WAS THOUGHT TO BE GOOD FOR EVERYONE. Radium was highly effective at killing cancer during that era, and this was mistakenly perceived as being good for everyone. If it can kill cancer, it can make you live longer! It wasn’t really understood that cancer treatments work because they destroy everything in the affected area, including the good tissue. . . 2.) PRELIMINARY TESTING OF RADIUM SHOWED A POSITIVE EFFECT ON THE HUMAN BODY. Preliminary testing showed that radium stimulated bone marrow to produce extra blood cells. I am assuming that back then this was considered a good thing, because in reality an excess of red blood cells is actually really bad for you. If there's an excess of red blood cells, it leads to a higher risk of blood clots. Returning to the past - in the beginning, the thought that radium is good for you wasn’t necessarily “scientifically unsound." It was just a case of not knowing the long term effects yet. Unfortunately, the reason WHY the bone marrow was stimulated is because it was being bombarded by ionizing radiation. Radioactive ionization never stops until it’s depleted. Radioactive substances emit crazy high energy until they degrade. This means that the gamma rays keep going, and eventually the things around it are affected. With radium and bones, the radium kept going until the bone simply ceased to be. . . 3.) RADIUM QUACKERY WAS HIGHLY LUCRATIVE Scientists quickly realized their deadly error on radium's "medicinal properties", but the word about the “health benefits” of radium had already been out, and it was making MILLIONS. Businesses were scrambling to get their fingers into this highly lucrative pie. The products flew off the shelves, sold by seedy conmen who knew better. . Of course, radium consumption is deadly whether you know it will kill you or not. The radium health fad stopped dead in its tracks when a well known and liked customer died from it.
When a famous and beloved man's jaw falls off of his skull, you can't really refute the evidence of radium's harmful effects. Having been backed into a corner, radium companies were forced to shut down. Most radium products stopped being made and sold except for watch dials, as there was no other alternative glow in the dark dial at that time. Eventually radium dials were banned as well under The Radiation Control For Health and Safety Act in 1968.
The famous picture associated with Eben Byers is actually a trauma caused by impact with an explosive. I couldn't find a picture of anyone with a missing jaw due to radium exposure, but I did find one with phossy jaw, which had the same effect. *Phosphorus exposure can also cause the jaw to erode until it falls out. A lot of people that worked in match factories would later have issues with eroded jaws due to their time in the factory. In fact, when the radium factory workers started presenting their symptoms, some doctors thought it was phossy jaw. This was confusing to everyone because the radium factory workers never worked with phosphorus. We'll get into that next time.
The girls would create a fine point with their brushes by applying it to their lips in a method called "lip, dip, paint." We'll go more into that next time. The point is, the regulations that require safe storage of radium were written in the paint that destroyed these women's bones.
There were more factory workers that suffered from radium exposure. These eighteen women were involved in a lawsuit against the American Radium Company suing for damages related to their injuries. It was a hell of a fight. Especially since the company knew that if they dragged their feet on the lawsuit, the plaintiffs would be dead and unable to sue them anymore. It was a race against a greedy CEO that had a hold of the clock. The joys of capitalism, eh?
Types of ionizing radiation: https://www.mirion.com/discover/knowledge-hub/articles/education/types-of-ionizing-radiation#:~:text=X%2Drays%20are%20similar%20to,than%20gamma%20radiation%2C%20as%20well. Parts of an atom: https://phys.org/news/2015-12-atom.html Properties of radiation: https://www.gla.ac.uk/media/Media_557410_smxx.pdf Another source on radioactive elements: https://www.healthvermont.gov/environment/radiological-health/radioactive-elements Radiation basics: https://www.nrc.gov/about-nrc/radiation/health-effects/radiation-basics.html Calcium and bones: https://healthybonesaustralia.org.au/your-bone-health/calcium/#:~:text=Why%20is%20calcium%20important?,at%20risk%20of%20developing%20osteoporosis. Calcium Metal Image Source: https://www.luciteria.com/elements-for-sale/buy-calcium Alkaline Earth Metals: https://www.diamond.ac.uk/Campaigns/2019/Year-of-the-Periodic-Table/Alkaline-Earth-Metals.html#:~:text=The%20alkaline%20earth%20metals%20are,a%20pH%20greater%20than%207. Radium and Medicine: https://radioactivity.eu.com/articles/phenomenon/radium_medicine Britannica had a great article on radium: https://www.britannica.com/science/radium Soldier’s Image: https://archive.org/details/in.ernet.dli.2015.553049 Source of Unknown Jaw Sample: https://iancpilarczyk.com/the-radium-girls-deodands-and-the-rise-of-workers-compensation/ The Initial Belief in Radium’s Healing Properties: https://open.spotify.com/show/6ZOoOKusMhgCpZSqouxlgp Skull Image Source: https://candent.ca/products/human-skull-44-female-html Phossy Jaw: https://www.rcseng.ac.uk/library-and-publications/library/blog/phossy-jaw-and-the-matchgirls/#:~:text=Phosphorus%20necrosis%20of%20the%20jaw,and%20sometimes%20fatal%20brain%20damage. Radiation Control and Safety Act: https://www.fda.gov/radiation-emitting-products/electronic-product-radiation-control-program/laws-and-regulations-radiation-emitting-products Illinois Occupational Diseases Act: https://iwcc.illinois.gov/content/dam/soi/en/web/iwcc/about/occupationaldiseasesact/documents/workersoccupationaldiseasesact.pdf There is a British Journal of Radiology 1998 Article that I read. The URL is INSANELY long, so I tried to use the URL of an image taken from the PDF, which would lead you to the article. That did not work, and now I can’t find the link to it at all and I’m frustrated.

26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @huleeb does tons of great work and this is one of them. Check out their posts for other amazing things.
27 Man Tax: Jon Batiste
“Life is a mystery to be lived, not a problem to be solved.” - Søren Kierkegaard So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 ***Also if you miss abbiistabbii's discord community good news! Here's the new one https://discord.gg/NPStJGgARq - where everyone's reconnecting, shitposting, and hanging out again*** https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. ***Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, DM me
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Federal agents with ICE detained a 16-year-old asylum-seeker in Minnesota and quietly transferred him out of state after labeling him an"unaccompanied minor," even though his father lives locally.
item.description: The agency has expanded its practice of moving children into a shadowy national shelter network. The teen was sent to a Michigan facility run by Bethany Christian Services, a federally contracted child services organization with financial ties to the DeVos family. While in detention the minor's family and attorneys struggled to locate him and were initially told by authorities they had no record of his custody. While in custody, the teen reported being injected with multiple vaccines in both arms without being told what they were. These abductions, carried out under the broader immigration crackdown overseen by the Department of Homeland Security during the Trump administration, reflects an enforcement strategy that enriches oligarchs like Betsy DeVos. DeVos and others' involvement shows Michigan’s religious nonprofit sector and the growing role of faith-based contractors in federal immigration detention and the limited transparency surrounding how and why children are moved far from their families.
item.dc:creator: jee22

item.title: I'm sort of hoping the tidal wave of lawsuits coming will eat up massive resources within the admin or DOJ, etc.
item.dc:creator: unclefishbits
